sop001.title: SOP-001 – Priming a New Construction House
sop001.content:
PURPOSE
To establish a consistent, efficient, and high-quality process for priming drywall in new construction homes.

NON-NEGOTIABLES
• We DO NOT brush bare drywall. Ever.
• Walls are rolled first. No cutting first.
• All belongings, windows, fixtures, and hardware must be protected.
• Safety check occurs before work begins.

PROCEDURE

1. Safety & Site Readiness
• Confirm site is safe and free of hazards.
• Confirm ventilation if spraying.
• Protect windows, hinges, countertops, fixtures, floors, and non-painted surfaces.

2. Wall Priming
• Roll all walls with drywall primer.
• Do not cut first.
• Maintain wet edge and even coverage.

3. Ceiling Priming
Option A – Spray & Back-Roll (Preferred):
• Spray ceilings evenly.
• Back-roll with 20 mil roller.
• Spray all corners and angles normally brushed.

Option B – Roll + Spray Corners:
• Roll ceilings with 20 mil roller.
• Spray all vertical and horizontal corners afterward.

FINAL CHECK
• No missed areas.
• No overspray.
• Uniform primer coverage.

sop002.title: SOP-002 – Painting Ceilings (New Construction, Primed)
sop002.content:
PURPOSE
To produce a finished ceiling surface using spray and back-roll with minimal coats.

NON-NEGOTIABLES
• All drywall must be sanded.
• Spray and back-roll only.
• 20 mil roller only.
• Protection is mandatory.

PROCEDURE
1. Sand ceilings and remove dust.
2. Protect windows, walls, fixtures, and floors.
3. Spray ceilings evenly.
4. Back-roll immediately with 20 mil roller.
5. Aim for one-coat finish; second coat only if required.

sop003.title: SOP-003 – Interior Painting Sequence (New Construction)
sop003.content:
MANDATORY ORDER
1. Prime walls and ceilings
2. Paint ceilings
3. Paint walls
4. Paint trim, doors, and frames

RULES
• No out-of-sequence work.
• Each stage must be inspected before proceeding.

sop004.title: SOP-004 – Painting Walls (Post-Ceiling)
sop004.content:
NON-NEGOTIABLES
• Ceilings must be complete.
• Protection remains in place.
• No free-styling application methods.

PROCEDURE
• Inspect walls for damage; patch and spot-prime as needed.
• Option A: Spray and back-roll (12–15 mil).
• Option B: Roll only when spraying is not approved.
• One full coat minimum; second coat if required.

sop005.title: SOP-005 – Bathroom & Facility Use Policy
sop005.content:
GENERAL POLICY
• Employees are strictly prohibited from using customer bathrooms, sinks, tubs, or drains.
• No exceptions.

BATHROOM BREAK SCHEDULE
• Approved break times: 10:00 AM, 12:00 PM, 2:00 PM.
• Use public or approved facilities only.

PROHIBITED PRACTICES
• No outdoor urination (bushes, woods, vehicles).
• No flushing wipes, feminine products, paper towels.
• Only toilet paper in toilets.

DISCIPLINE
• First offense: written warning.
• Repeat offenses: suspension or termination.

sop006.title: SOP-006 – Equipment Storage & Vehicle Use
sop006.content:
POLICY
• Vehicles are mobile storage units.
• Only bring tools needed for the task.
• Do not unload full vehicles into job sites.
• No tools left on site overnight (except labeled paint and tray liners).

END OF DAY
• Tools returned to vehicle or shop.
• Vehicles locked.

sop007.title: SOP-007 – Equipment Sign-Out & Sundries Tracking
sop007.content:
EQUIPMENT
• All tools must be signed out.
• Condition logged out and in.
• Missing/damaged tools reported immediately.

SUNDRIES
• All consumables logged when taken from shop.
• Weekly review and monthly audits.

sop008.title: SOP-008 – Driving, Transportation, Travel Time & Vehicle Use (Ontario)
sop008.content:
LEGAL BASIS
• Commuting is unpaid.
• Employer-directed travel is paid.
• Company vehicles/fuel do not make commuting paid.

CORE RULES
• Clock in at job site only.
• Never clock in from home.
• GPS/geofence enforced.

SHOP EXCEPTIONS
• Only instructed driver clocks in at shop.
• Shop-to-job travel is paid.

CARPOOLING
• Personal choice.
• Driver not paid.
• Riders do not work or load vehicles.

PICKUPS
• Employer-directed pickups are paid.
• Clock in at first required stop.

END OF DAY
• Clock out when work ends.
• Travel home unpaid.

COMPANY VEHICLES
• Personal use is a privilege.
• No paid commuting.
• Fuel for personal use is a bonus.

ROOMMATES
• Living together does not create paid travel.
• Disputes require personal transportation.

FOREMAN RIDE POLICY
• Foremen are not chauffeurs.
• No paid extra trips for employee convenience.
• Shop → Job only when directed.

NO SMOKING OR VAPING
• No smoking or vaping in company vehicles.
• Applies at all times.
• Violations result in loss of privileges and discipline.

sop009.title: SOP-009 – Vacuum Use, Dust Control & Safety
sop009.content:
PURPOSE
To establish safe, effective, and professional procedures for the use, maintenance, cleaning, storage, and selection of vacuum systems on job sites and in the shop.

GENERAL POLICY
• Always use a vacuum with an appropriate filter and a filter bag when vacuuming dry debris, dust, or fine particles.
• Vacuuming without a filter or bag is prohibited except for specific wet applications.

EQUIPMENT SELECTION
• Use the correct vacuum for the task:
  – Large construction vacuums (e.g., Festool or HEPA-equipped units) are for sanding drywall and other heavy dust-generating tasks.
  – Smaller shop vacs may be used for light cleanup and non-hazardous debris.
• Do not use a standard shop vacuum for fine drywall dust or hazardous dust without a proper HEPA-rated filter.

FILTERS & BAGS
• A vacuum must always have:
  – An intact filter (correct type for the material)
  – A filter bag installed
• Never operate dry vacuum equipment without both a filter and a bag installed.
• Exception: When vacuuming water or liquids, remove the dry-use bag and filter. After wet use, clean, dry, and reinstall the appropriate dry dust filter and bag.

SAFETY & DUST CONTROL
• Dust generated from sanding, grinding, or cutting can contain respirable particles that are harmful when inhaled.
• For tasks generating fine dust, use HEPA-rated vacuums that capture particles as small as 0.3 microns.
• Whenever possible, attach the vacuum directly to the tool generating dust.
• Never use compressed air to blow dust off surfaces.

sop010.title: SOP-010 – Orbital & Sheet Sander Use, Paper Selection, Cleaning & Storage
sop010.content:
PURPOSE
To ensure high-quality finishes, effective dust extraction, long tool life, and safe operation of orbital and sheet sanders.

NON-NEGOTIABLES
• Always use the correct brand of sandpaper with the matching sander.
• Festool sandpaper is used ONLY on Festool sanders.
• Generic sandpaper is used ONLY on generic sanders.
• Sandpaper holes MUST align with the sander pad holes.
• Sanders MUST be connected to a vacuum or dust collection system when required.
• Sanders and all parts MUST be stored in their proper bag or box.
• Never store used, worn, or dirty sandpaper in the sander storage container.

SANDPAPER SELECTION
• Use only sandpaper designed for the specific sander brand and model.
• Hole patterns must line up exactly with the sanding pad.
• Always use the correct grit for the task:
  – Coarse grits for leveling and heavy material removal
  – Medium grits for general prep
  – Fine grits for finish sanding

PROPER SANDING PRACTICES
• Always keep sandpaper clean and free of debris.
• Stop immediately if paper becomes clogged or contaminated.
• Never continue sanding with dirty paper.
• Do not press hard on the sander.
• Keep the sander flat on the surface at all times.
• Lift the sander off the surface before stopping to avoid swirl marks.

STORAGE (NON-NEGOTIABLE)
• Every sander must be stored in its designated bag or case.
• Store with power cord properly wrapped.
• NEVER throw sanders loose into vans or store without their case.

sop011.title: SOP-011 – General Tool Maintenance, Cleaning, Inspection & Storage
sop011.content:
PURPOSE
To ensure all tools used by the company are clean, safe, functional, and ready for use at all times.

CORE RULES (NON-NEGOTIABLE)
• All tools must be cleaned after use.
• All tools must be inspected before use.
• All tools must be stored properly.
• Employees must arrive at work with clean, ready-to-use tools.
• Damaged or unsafe tools must not be used.

DAILY TOOL CLEANING – END OF USE

Roller Cages & Frames:
• Clean immediately after use.
• Remove all wet paint.
• If paint dries on a cage, it must be fully scraped and cleaned before reuse.

Roller Covers:
• Wash thoroughly until water runs clear.
• Spin out excess water.
• Allow to dry fully before storage.

Brushes:
• Clean brushes daily.
• Remove all paint from bristles and ferrule.
• Shape bristles properly before drying.
• Employees must arrive to work with clean brushes.

DAILY INSPECTION – BEFORE USE
Before using any tool, inspect for:
• Power cord cuts, frays, exposed wires
• Loose plugs or strain relief damage
• Cracked housings
• Excessive vibration
• Proper switch operation

STORAGE (NON-NEGOTIABLE)
• All tools must be stored clean and dry.
• Store tools in designated bins, racks, bags, or cases.
• Electric tools must be stored with cords wrapped properly.
• Do not throw tools loose into vans or job sites.

sop012.title: SOP-012 – Paint Sprayer Use, Cleaning, Maintenance & Tip Management
sop012.content:
PURPOSE
To ensure all paint sprayers are used correctly, cleaned thoroughly, maintained properly, and stored safely.

CORE RULES (NON-NEGOTIABLE)
• Sprayers must be cleaned after EVERY use.
• No sprayer is left dirty overnight.
• Tips, filters, and guns must be cleaned immediately.
• Sprayers must be flushed properly before storage.

PRE-USE INSPECTION (BEFORE EVERY USE)
Inspect the following before spraying:

Sprayer Unit:
• No leaks
• No abnormal noises
• Proper pressure response
• Clean intake screen

Hoses:
• No cuts, bubbles, kinks, or soft spots
• Proper fittings, tight connections

Gun & Guard:
• Trigger functions properly
• Guard installed correctly
• No paint buildup affecting spray pattern

TIP SELECTION & USE
• Use the correct tip size for material type, viscosity, and surface.
• Never force material through an incorrect tip.
• Tips are consumables – replace if spraying unevenly.

CLEANING PROCEDURE (MANDATORY)
Step 1 – Empty Material:
• Remove remaining paint from hopper or bucket.
• Return usable paint to labeled container.

Step 2 – Flush System:
• Flush with appropriate cleaner (water for water-based, solvent for solvent-based).
• Flush until fluid runs completely clear.

Step 3 – Gun, Tip & Filter Cleaning:
• Remove spray tip and guard.
• Clean thoroughly with brush and cleaner.

STORAGE (NON-NEGOTIABLE)
• Store sprayers clean and dry.
• Use pump protectant or storage fluid as required.
• Coil hoses properly—no kinks.
• Never store sprayers with paint inside.

SAFETY
• High-pressure spray can inject paint into skin (medical emergency).
• Never point gun at people.
• Always engage trigger lock when not spraying.
• Wear appropriate PPE.

sop013.title: SOP-013 – Prep & Surface Protection (Masking & Containment)
sop013.content:
PURPOSE
To protect customer property, finished surfaces, and work areas from damage, dust, overspray, and contamination.

NON-NEGOTIABLES
• Protection happens BEFORE prep or painting.
• No spraying or sanding without protection in place.
• Assume everything can be damaged unless protected.

PROCEDURE

1. Floors
• Cover all floors with drop sheets, ram board, or poly as required.
• Tape seams securely.
• No exposed walking paths.

2. Walls, Fixtures & Surfaces
• Mask windows, glass, hardware, cabinets, appliances, counters.
• Cover HVAC registers, smoke detectors, and vents when spraying.
• Remove plates, fixtures, and hardware where possible.

3. Containment
• Use plastic walls or zip walls when sanding or spraying.
• Seal doorways and transitions.
• Maintain negative air if required.

4. Daily Check
• Inspect protection daily.
• Repair or replace damaged coverings immediately.

Failure to protect is considered negligence.

sop014.title: SOP-014 – End-of-Day Jobsite Shutdown
sop014.content:
PURPOSE
To leave every jobsite safe, clean, secure, and professional at the end of each day.

PROCEDURE

1. Work Area
• Stop work with enough time to clean properly.
• No rushed cleanups.

2. Tools & Materials
• Tools cleaned and stored in vehicles or approved site storage.
• No loose tools left out.
• Paint sealed, labeled, and stored safely.

3. Cleanup
• Vacuum all work areas.
• Remove debris and garbage.
• Wipe down surfaces as needed.

4. Safety & Security
• Remove tripping hazards.
• Lock doors and windows if instructed.
• Shut off lights and equipment.
• Set thermostats per site instructions.

5. Final Walk
• Foreman or lead does a final walkthrough.
• Site must be presentable to customer or trades.

sop015.title: SOP-015 – Foreman Daily Checklist
sop015.content:
PURPOSE
To ensure consistency, productivity, safety, and communication on every job.

DAILY RESPONSIBILITIES

Morning:
• Confirm crew attendance.
• Review scope of work.
• Confirm materials and tools.
• Inspect site safety and protection.

During the Day:
• Monitor quality and sequence.
• Ensure SOP compliance.
• Address issues immediately.
• Communicate changes or delays.

End of Day:
• Review progress.
• Complete daily report.
• Take photos if required.
• Ensure jobsite shutdown SOP is followed.

Foremen are accountable for SOP enforcement.

sop016.title: SOP-016 – Quality Control & Punch-List
sop016.content:
PURPOSE
To deliver consistent, professional finishes and minimize callbacks.

QUALITY STANDARDS
• Even coverage
• No runs, sags, or lap marks
• Clean lines
• No missed areas
• No damage to surrounding surfaces

INSPECTION PROCESS

1. Self-Check
• Painters inspect their own work before moving on.

2. Foreman Check
• Inspect under proper lighting.
• Mark deficiencies immediately.

3. Punch-List
• Create clear list of fixes.
• Assign responsibility.
• Complete before moving to next phase.

No job is considered complete until punch-list is cleared.

sop017.title: SOP-017 – Damage Prevention & Property Protection
sop017.content:
PURPOSE
To prevent damage to customer property, finishes, fixtures, and surrounding areas.

RULES
• Treat every home as occupied and finished.
• Never lean tools against walls or furniture.
• Never drag tools or hoses across finished surfaces.
• Never place tools on counters or furniture.

LADDERS & EQUIPMENT
• Pad ladder contact points.
• Use drop cloths under ladders.
• Do not rest ladders on finished trim or cabinets.

INCIDENTS
• Any damage must be reported immediately.
• Do not hide or ignore damage.
• Complete incident report same day.

Failure to report damage is a serious violation.

sop018.title: SOP-018 – Foreman Authority & Decision-Making
sop018.content:
PURPOSE
To clearly define the authority, responsibility, and decision-making power of foremen on job sites to prevent delays, confusion, and scope creep.

AUTHORITY
• The foreman is the highest authority on site unless otherwise stated.
• All crew members must follow foreman instructions.
• Foremen enforce all SOPs without exception.

DECISION RULES
Foremen MAY decide:
• Daily work sequencing
• Tool and material usage
• Minor workflow adjustments
• Quality corrections
• Safety stoppages

Foremen MAY NOT decide:
• Price changes
• Scope changes
• Free extras for customers
• Deviations from written SOPs
• Ignoring damage or incidents

ESCALATION
• Any scope, cost, or customer request outside the contract must be escalated to Chad or management immediately.
• When in doubt: STOP and ASK.

Failure to follow foreman direction is insubordination.

sop019.title: SOP-019 – Customer Interaction & Communication
sop019.content:
PURPOSE
To ensure professional, consistent, and controlled communication with customers.

GENERAL RULES
• Be polite, professional, and brief.
• Do not overshare information.
• Do not speculate or guess.
• Do not promise anything.

WHAT EMPLOYEES MAY SAY
• What work is scheduled today
• General progress updates
• That questions will be passed to management

WHAT EMPLOYEES MAY NOT SAY
• Pricing or discounts
• Schedule guarantees
• Opinions on other trades
• Criticism of the home or prior work
• Authorization for extra work

CUSTOMER REQUESTS
• All change requests must be directed to the foreman.
• The foreman escalates to management.
• No verbal approvals.

sop020.title: SOP-020 – Change Orders
sop020.content:
PURPOSE
To control scope, pricing, and approval of work outside the original agreement.

RULES
• No extra work is performed without a written change order.
• Verbal approvals are invalid.
• "While you're here" work is not allowed.

PROCESS
1. Customer requests change.
2. Foreman documents request.
3. Management prices change.
4. Customer signs approval.
5. Work is scheduled and completed.

Unauthorized work will not be paid.

sop021.title: SOP-021 – Safety at Heights & Ladder Use
sop021.content:
PURPOSE
To prevent falls and injuries when working at height.

RULES
• Inspect ladders daily.
• Use the correct ladder for the task.
• Never stand on the top step.
• Maintain three points of contact.
• Do not overreach.

SETUP
• Place ladders on stable ground.
• Use ladder feet or levelers.
• Secure ladders where required.

PROHIBITED
• Broken or damaged ladders
• Standing on buckets, boxes, or makeshift platforms
• Carrying heavy items while climbing

Violations may result in removal from site.

sop022.title: SOP-022 – Disciplinary Matrix & Enforcement
sop022.content:
PURPOSE
To ensure consistent, fair enforcement of all SOPs.

DISCIPLINE LEVELS

Level 1 – Verbal Warning
• Minor SOP violations
• First-time offenses

Level 2 – Written Warning
• Repeat violations
• Safety issues
• Tool neglect

Level 3 – Suspension
• Serious violations
• Damage concealment
• Time theft

Level 4 – Termination
• Willful safety violations
• Dishonesty
• Repeated insubordination

NOTES
• Discipline may skip levels depending on severity.
• All incidents are documented.
• Ignorance of SOPs is not an excuse.

sop023.title: SOP-023 – New Construction Site Etiquette & Conduct
sop023.content:
PURPOSE
To ensure professional behavior, efficiency, and respect on all new construction job sites where multiple trades are present.

RULES
• We are guests on the builder's site.
• Follow site rules, signage, and safety requirements at all times.
• Do not interfere with other trades.
• Do not touch, move, or use other trades' tools or materials.

CONDUCT
• No loud music.
• No swearing, yelling, or inappropriate language.
• No wandering into areas outside our scope.
• No sitting on finished surfaces.

CLEANLINESS
• Keep work area clean at all times.
• Do not leave debris for other trades.
• Clean up daily per End-of-Day SOP.

Violations will be reported to management and may result in removal from site.

sop024.title: SOP-024 – Prep Standards by Surface Type
sop024.content:
PURPOSE
To standardize surface preparation to ensure adhesion, durability, and finish quality.

DRYWALL
• Sand all joints and repairs.
• Remove dust before priming.
• Spot-prime all patches.

WOOD (TRIM, DOORS, FRAMES)
• Sand smooth.
• Fill nail holes.
• Caulk gaps.
• Spot-prime bare wood.

PREVIOUSLY PAINTED SURFACES
• Clean to remove dust, grease, or residue.
• Sand to dull sheen.
• Repair defects before coating.

METAL
• Remove rust.
• Degrease surface.
• Prime with appropriate metal primer.

No paint is applied without proper prep.

sop025.title: SOP-025 – Paint Handling, Mixing & Storage
sop025.content:
PURPOSE
To ensure paint quality, consistency, and safety.

HANDLING
• Store paint upright with lids sealed.
• Label all paint with room, color, sheen, and date.
• Do not leave paint exposed or open.

MIXING
• Stir thoroughly before use.
• Box paint when required to ensure consistency.
• Do not thin unless approved.

STORAGE
• Store paint in temperature-controlled areas.
• Protect from freezing or overheating.
• Dispose of unusable paint properly.

SPILLS
• Clean immediately.
• Report large spills.

sop026.title: SOP-026 – Incident Investigation & Reporting
sop026.content:
PURPOSE
To ensure incidents are reported, documented, and corrected.

INCIDENTS INCLUDE
• Injury
• Property damage
• Near misses
• Equipment failure
• Safety violations

PROCESS
1. Stop work if unsafe.
2. Secure area.
3. Notify foreman immediately.
4. Complete incident report same day.
5. Take photos if applicable.

No retaliation for reporting incidents.
Failure to report is a serious violation.

sop027.title: SOP-027 – Safety Orientation & Jobsite Safety
sop027.content:
PURPOSE
To ensure all employees understand basic safety expectations.

REQUIREMENTS
• Attend safety orientation.
• Understand site-specific hazards.
• Wear required PPE.
• Follow all safety SOPs.

GENERAL SAFETY
• Keep walkways clear.
• Report hazards immediately.
• Use tools only as trained.

RIGHT TO REFUSE
• Employees may refuse unsafe work.
• Report concerns to foreman or management.

Safety violations may result in immediate removal from site.

sop028.title: SOP-028 – Employee Onboarding & Probation
sop028.content:
PURPOSE
To ensure new hires are trained, compliant, and evaluated before full integration.

ONBOARDING REQUIREMENTS
• Review all SOPs
• Sign acknowledgments
• Safety orientation completed
• Tool, vehicle, and conduct expectations reviewed

PROBATION PERIOD
• First 30–90 days are probationary
• Performance, attitude, punctuality, and SOP compliance evaluated
• Probation may be extended or employment terminated at management discretion

Failure to follow SOPs during probation may result in immediate termination.

sop029.title: SOP-029 – Foreman Daily Report & Photo Standards
sop029.content:
PURPOSE
To document progress, issues, and quality daily.

DAILY REPORT MUST INCLUDE
• Job name and date
• Crew on site
• Work completed
• Issues or delays
• Materials used
• Safety or damage incidents

PHOTO STANDARDS
• Start-of-day photos
• Mid-day progress photos
• End-of-day photos
• Photos must be clear and labeled

Reports must be completed daily before clock-out.

sop030.title: SOP-030 – Warranty & Touch-Up Work
sop030.content:
PURPOSE
To manage warranty claims and touch-up work professionally and efficiently.

RULES
• Warranty work must be approved by management.
• No unauthorized touch-ups.
• Document issue with photos and notes.

PROCESS
1. Receive warranty request.
2. Inspect and document.
3. Management approval.
4. Schedule repair.
5. Complete and document repair.

Warranty work is not free unless approved.

sop031.title: SOP-031 – Weather & Environmental Conditions
sop031.content:
PURPOSE
To ensure coatings perform correctly under proper conditions.

RULES
• Follow manufacturer temperature and humidity limits.
• No exterior painting in rain, snow, or freezing conditions.
• Monitor weather daily.

INDOOR CONDITIONS
• Ensure proper ventilation.
• Control dust and airflow.

Foremen may stop work if conditions are unsuitable.

sop032.title: SOP-032 – Production Rates & Efficiency
sop032.content:
PURPOSE
To maintain productivity and profitability.

EXPECTATIONS
• Employees are expected to meet reasonable production rates.
• Work steadily and efficiently.
• Excessive downtime is not acceptable.

MONITORING
• Foremen track progress daily.
• Repeated inefficiency will be addressed.

Quality is never sacrificed for speed.

sop033.title: SOP-033 – Material Ordering & Inventory Control
sop033.content:
PURPOSE
To ensure materials are ordered accurately, inventory is tracked, shortages are avoided, and waste is minimized.

ORDERING
• Materials are ordered only by foremen or management.
• Orders must match approved specs (brand, color, sheen, quantity).
• No last-minute or unauthorized substitutions.

INVENTORY
• Log all materials taken from the shop.
• Update inventory upon return of unused materials.
• Clearly label partial containers with job name and date.

WASTE
• Do not over-order.
• Return usable materials to inventory.
• Dispose of unusable materials properly.

sop034.title: SOP-034 – Shop Operations & Opening/Closing
sop034.content:
PURPOSE
To keep the shop clean, safe, organized, and ready for daily operations.

OPENING
• Unlock doors and disarm alarm.
• Turn on lights as needed.
• Check shop cleanliness and safety.
• Confirm equipment readiness.

CLOSING
• Clean work areas.
• Put tools, sundries, and paint away.
• Empty garbage and recycling.
• Clean bathroom.
• Turn off lights and equipment.
• Set thermostat per policy.
• Lock doors and arm alarm.

The shop must be left clean every day.

sop035.title: SOP-035 – Client Walkthrough & Project Handover
sop035.content:
PURPOSE
To ensure a professional handover and reduce callbacks.

PROCESS
• Foreman schedules walkthrough.
• Inspect all work before client review.
• Address concerns calmly and professionally.
• Document any punch-list items.

HANDOVER
• Complete punch-list.
• Clean area thoroughly.
• Obtain sign-off when possible.

No project is complete without proper handover.

sop036.title: SOP-036 – Emergency Response
sop036.content:
PURPOSE
To respond quickly and safely to emergencies.

EMERGENCIES INCLUDE
• Injury
• Fire
• Flood
• Chemical spill
• Structural hazard

PROCEDURE
1. Stop work.
2. Ensure personal safety.
3. Call emergency services if required.
4. Notify foreman immediately.
5. Secure area.
6. Complete incident report.

Do not resume work until cleared.

sop037.title: SOP-037 – Documentation, File Naming & Data Storage
sop037.content:
PURPOSE
To ensure consistent, traceable documentation.

FILE NAMING
• JobName_Date_Description
• Example: Smith_St_2025-03-12_DailyReport

STORAGE
• Upload files to designated folders only.
• No personal devices for company records.
• Photos must be clear and dated.

RETENTION
• Do not delete company records.
• Follow retention policies.

sop038.title: SOP-038 – Confined Space Awareness (Painting Operations)
sop038.content:
PURPOSE
To ensure employees recognize confined spaces and avoid unsafe entry without proper authorization and controls.

DEFINITION
A confined space is an area that:
• Is not designed for continuous occupancy
• Has limited entry/exit
• May contain hazards (poor air, fumes, dust)

EXAMPLES
• Crawl spaces
• Attics with limited access
• Mechanical rooms
• Tanks or pits

RULES
• Employees do NOT enter confined spaces unless specifically trained and authorized.
• No sanding, spraying, or coating in confined spaces without approval.
• If unsure, STOP and contact the foreman.

sop039.title: SOP-039 – Hazardous Materials & SDS (Safety Data Sheets)
sop039.content:
PURPOSE
To ensure safe handling of hazardous materials.

RULES
• All hazardous products must have an SDS available.
• Employees must review SDS before use.
• Follow PPE and ventilation requirements.

HANDLING
• Store chemicals properly.
• Do not mix products unless approved.
• Label all containers clearly.

SPILLS
• Stop work.
• Contain spill.
• Notify foreman immediately.

sop040.title: SOP-040 – Noise & Hearing Protection
sop040.content:
PURPOSE
To protect hearing from excessive noise exposure.

RULES
• Hearing protection required when using loud equipment:
  – Sanders
  – Sprayers
  – Compressors
  – Power tools
• Use earplugs or earmuffs as required.
• Replace damaged hearing protection.

PROHIBITED
• Ignoring hearing protection requirements.
• Modifying equipment to increase noise.

sop041.title: SOP-041 – Electrical Safety (Tools & Power)
sop041.content:
PURPOSE
To prevent electrical injuries and equipment damage.

RULES
• Inspect cords daily.
• Do not use damaged cords or tools.
• Keep cords away from water and traffic areas.
• Use GFCI protection where required.

PROHIBITED
• Overloading outlets.
• Using makeshift repairs.
• Running cords through water.

sop042.title: SOP-042 – Annual Training, Review & Certification
sop042.content:
PURPOSE
To ensure ongoing compliance and skill development.

REQUIREMENTS
• Annual review of SOPs.
• Annual safety training.
• Tool and equipment refresher training.
• Documentation of completed training.

FAILURE
• Failure to complete training may result in removal from site or discipline.

sop043.title: SOP-043 – Lead Paint & Renovation Awareness
sop043.content:
PURPOSE
To protect workers and occupants from exposure to lead-containing materials during renovation and repainting.

RULES
• Assume pre-1990 buildings may contain lead paint.
• Do not disturb suspected lead paint without approval.
• No dry sanding, grinding, or scraping of suspect materials.
• Use containment and dust control at all times.

IF LEAD IS SUSPECTED
• Stop work immediately.
• Notify foreman and management.
• Do not resume until cleared.

Only trained personnel may perform lead-related work.

sop044.title: SOP-044 – Respirator Use, Fit & Care
sop044.content:
PURPOSE
To ensure respirators are used correctly to protect employee health.

RULES
• Use respirators when required by task or SDS.
• Employees must be fit-tested.
• Facial hair that interferes with seal is prohibited.

CARE
• Clean respirators after each use.
• Store in sealed, clean containers.
• Inspect before each use.

Damaged or poorly fitting respirators must not be used.

sop045.title: SOP-045 – Personal Protective Equipment (PPE)
sop045.content:
PURPOSE
To establish minimum PPE standards.

REQUIRED PPE (AS APPLICABLE)
• Safety glasses
• Gloves
• Respirators
• Hearing protection
• Protective clothing

RULES
• PPE must be worn when required.
• PPE must be clean and in good condition.
• Do not modify PPE.

Failure to wear PPE is a safety violation.

sop046.title: SOP-046 – Jobsite Signage & Barricading
sop046.content:
PURPOSE
To protect workers, customers, and the public.

RULES
• Use signage to identify hazards.
• Barricade sanding or spraying areas.
• Restrict access to work zones.
• Remove signage only when hazards are cleared.

Do not rely on verbal warnings alone.

sop047.title: SOP-047 – Subcontractor Management
sop047.content:
PURPOSE
To ensure subcontractors meet company standards.

REQUIREMENTS
• Subcontractors must follow all SOPs.
• Provide proof of insurance and safety compliance.
• Work must be inspected by foreman.

VIOLATIONS
• Unsafe or unprofessional conduct may result in removal from site.
• Subcontractors do not override company SOPs.

sop048.title: SOP-048 – Environmental Protection & Waste Disposal
sop048.content:
PURPOSE
To ensure all waste is handled responsibly, legally, and professionally while protecting the environment and customer property.

RULES
• Dispose of all waste in approved containers only.
• Separate garbage, recyclables, and hazardous waste.
• Never dump liquids, paint, or solvents down drains, toilets, or outside.

PAINT & COATINGS
• Do not pour paint into sinks, toilets, or onto the ground.
• Allow small amounts of water-based paint to dry before disposal if permitted.
• Solvent-based materials must be disposed of according to local regulations.

DUST & DEBRIS
• Vacuum dust; do not sweep into the air.
• Bag debris before disposal.

Violations may result in discipline and fines.

sop049.title: SOP-049 – Security & Theft Prevention
sop049.content:
PURPOSE
To protect company tools, materials, vehicles, and customer property.

RULES
• Lock vehicles at all times when unattended.
• Do not leave tools or materials unsecured.
• Do not allow unauthorized persons access to tools or vehicles.

JOB SITES
• Secure tools at end of day.
• Do not leave valuables visible.

REPORTING
• Report lost, missing, or stolen items immediately.
• Failure to report may result in disciplinary action.

sop050.title: SOP-050 – After-Hours & Site Access
sop050.content:
PURPOSE
To control access to job sites outside normal working hours.

RULES
• No after-hours access without approval.
• No bringing family, friends, or unauthorized persons to job sites.
• Keys, codes, and access credentials are confidential.

END OF DAY
• Lock doors and windows as instructed.
• Arm alarms if applicable.

Unauthorized access is grounds for discipline.

sop051.title: SOP-051 – Technology, Phones & Device Use
sop051.content:
PURPOSE
To minimize distractions and protect company data.

RULES
• Personal phone use is limited to breaks.
• No social media use during work hours.
• No unauthorized photos or videos.

COMPANY DEVICES
• Use company devices for work only.
• Protect passwords and login credentials.
• Report lost or damaged devices immediately.

Violation may result in loss of device privileges.

sop052.title: SOP-052 – Company Image, Uniform & Appearance
sop052.content:
PURPOSE
To present a professional image to customers, builders, and the public.

STANDARDS
• Wear clean company clothing or approved workwear.
• Clothing must be free of excessive paint, rips, or offensive content.
• Maintain basic personal hygiene.

ON SITE
• No inappropriate language.
• No smoking or vaping on site or in vehicles.
• Behave professionally at all times.

Employees represent the company at all times while on the job.
